question_text: In what year was the 340B Drug Pricing Program established?
explanation: The 340B program was created by Section 602 of the Veterans Health Care Act of 1992, codified at 42 U.S.C. § 256b.

question_text: Which federal agency administers the 340B Drug Pricing Program?

answers.text: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS)
Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA)
Food and Drug Administration (FDA)
Office of Inspector General (OIG)

explanation: HRSA's Office of Pharmacy Affairs (OPA) administers the 340B program and maintains OPAIS.

question_text: Which of the following is NOT a covered entity type eligible for the 340B program?

answers.text: Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC)
Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program grantee
For-profit independent pharmacy
Disproportionate Share Hospital (DSH)

explanation: For-profit independent pharmacies are not eligible covered entities. Covered entities are specific safety-net providers defined in the statute.

question_text: What is the primary benefit of 340B pricing for covered entities?

answers.text: Federal grant funding for pharmacy operations
Purchase of outpatient drugs at or below ceiling prices
Exemption from state pharmacy licensing
Priority access to drug shortage allocations

explanation: 340B allows covered entities to purchase covered outpatient drugs at or below a statutory ceiling price, stretching scarce federal resources.

answers.text: Office of Pharmacy Assistance and Integrated Services
Outpatient Pharmacy Access and Information System
Office of Pharmacy Affairs Information System
Outpatient Program Administration and Integrity System

explanation: OPAIS (Office of Pharmacy Affairs Information System) is the HRSA database where covered entities register and maintain their 340B program information.

question_text: Which statute is the primary legal basis for the 340B program?

answers.text: Social Security Act Section 1927
Public Health Service Act Section 340B (42 U.S.C. § 256b)
Affordable Care Act Section 3022
Drug Price Competition and Patent Term Restoration Act

explanation: The 340B program is codified at Section 340B of the Public Health Service Act, 42 U.S.C. § 256b.

question_text: A Disproportionate Share Hospital (DSH) must meet a minimum DSH adjustment percentage to qualify for 340B. What is that threshold?
explanation: DSH hospitals must have a Medicare DSH adjustment percentage greater than 11.75% to be eligible for 340B.

question_text: Which of the following drug types is generally NOT eligible as a covered outpatient drug under 340B?

answers.text: FDA-approved biologics dispensed to outpatients
Inpatient drugs used during an inpatient hospital stay
Specialty drugs dispensed to registered patients
Vaccines dispensed on an outpatient basis

explanation: Inpatient drugs (those reimbursed as part of an inpatient stay under PPS/DRG) are excluded. 340B applies to covered outpatient drugs only.
